text: Newton. “ Thank you .” Newton nodded. “The name you’ve chosen suits you well. Do not lose who you have become .” “I won’t,” Ronin replied, “and I hope you remember who you were, before too much longer .” Lara jiggled the broken handle and pushed the heavy door open. The metal-on-metal scrape of its hinges was like the groan of a dying animal, echoing off the concrete walls . The next sound, so small in comparison but so much more powerful, was Lara’s startled gasp. Ronin swung his optics upward to see hands closing on her arms, and then she was dragged outside his field of vision . “Ronin!” she screamed . He leapt up the stairs and burst into the yellow-gray morning, swinging his rifle into hand. Lara was near the house. Ronin didn’t waste any processing power contemplating how it had withstood the storm . Two gearheads held Lara, one with a large hand clamped over her mouth. Warlord stood beside them. Ronin took aim at him, but didn’t fire. The risk to Lara was too great . Wordlessly, the gearheads shifted Lara to Warlord’s hold. He wrapped one arm around her neck and the other around her waist, cutting off her scream by putting pressure on her throat. Her fingers clawed at his forearm. Wide-eyed, she met Ronin’s optics . “Put the gun down, dustwalker,” Warlord commanded flatly . Ronin hesitated, simulations running frantically, searching for some way to turn the situation to his favor. To save her . “You disobey, and you know how it ends. Don’t make me ask again .” Hovering near the trigger guard, Ronin’s finger twitched. Would one shot be enough to end it? To do what Lara wanted, to end Warlord for good, and free all the people who lived in his shadow of terror? Was this the sort of sacrifice she wanted them to make ? But he couldn’t risk her . He removed his left hand from the foregrip and crouched, reaching forward to place the rifle on the dusty ground . Warlord’s optics flicked infinitesimally from side to side. Ronin’s audio receptors picked up a whisper of grass, and then a great weight hit him from each side. Two more gearheads. They took hold of his arms, wrenching them behind his back, pulled off his packs, and forced him facedown into the dirt . “I prefer it when things go the easy way,” Warlord said as one of the other gearheads strode forward and plucked up the rifle. “This part was easy. Unfortunately, you made the rest of this very unpleasant for me. I don’t appreciate having to leave my city because my leniency’s been abused .” “You said to get rid of her. She’s not in Cheyenne anymore.” Ronin twisted his head to center Lara in his vision . “I did, didn’t I? I shouldn’t be surprised you took it that way. Whenever I think I’ve made something abundantly clear to you, it goes over your head. Guess I’ll just have to demonstrate what I mean when I tell you to get rid of something .” Warlord released Lara only long enough to clamp a hand on the back of her neck. His lip curled as he looked her over. “You should have stayed in the dirt, where you belong.” He slammed a fist into her abdomen . Lara doubled over, wheezing, and crumpled at Warlord’s feet. She convulsed and vomited, emptying the meager contents of her stomach onto the ground . Ronin’s processors went into overdrive, pouring power into his actuators for two tasks — destroying his enemies and protecting Lara. He surged up, and the gearheads restraining him stumbled away. One caught Ronin’s wrist as he started toward Warlord . Spinning, Ronin slammed his fist down on the bot’s outstretched arm, hitting its elbow joint from the side. Metal crunched as it bent in the wrong direction, breaking the gearhead’s hold. It was enough of a delay for the second gearhead to recover . The other bot slammed into Ronin from the side, knocking them to the ground. A cloud of dust obscured Ronin’s optics as he twisted to search for Lara . Strong hands grasped his head, forcing him to face Warlord. The weight of at least three bots settled atop Ronin, pinning him to the ground, their hands and feet pressing on his limbs . “All this trouble for a meatbag,” Warlord said as the dust settled . “Fuck you,” Lara spat. She held an arm around her midsection . “You already did.” Warlord kicked her ribs. She cried out, tumbling over the dust. “You could have made this painless for her, dustwalker, if you had listened to me .” Warlord’s stride was unhurried as he walked to Lara. He crouched before her, taking a fistful of her hair, and lifted her head from the dirt. Lara bared her teeth, a muted sound of pain rising from her throat . “Maybe I’ll let the rest of them fuck her before she’s done. Find out why you thought she was worth keeping .” Ronin had been slow to recognize his growing love for Lara, but he was learning hatred much faster. Lara was his life, his reason, his long-sought-after purpose. And Warlord meant to take her away. She was going to be killed, and Ronin couldn’t do anything to stop it . This was no matter of survival for Warlord. That thing — neither bot nor human — had nothing to gain or lose in this, no motivation apart from prejudice, cruelty, and pride. Despite the contempt blossoming in him, Ronin could not understand Warlord’s hatred; it was directed at a people he used to belong to . Ronin’s ire had a singular target and clear reasons. It was an emotion of cold logic, and, had he known it sooner, he would have ended Warlord at his earliest opportunity . Lara lashed out, fingers digging into the sutured gash on Warlord’s face. She
